_comment: WC2026 Group J, MD3 -- THE decider for 2nd place. Standings before: Argentina 6 (top), Austria 3 (3-1 Jordan, 0-2 Argentina, GD 0, GF 3), Algeria 3 (2-1 Jordan, 0-3 Argentina, GD -2, GF 2), Jordan 0. Argentina will almost surely beat Jordan and take 1st, so this match decides who is 2nd (qualifies directly) and who is 3rd. The 3rd-place team's fate then depends on the BEST-THIRDS table: the current 8th/last qualifying third is South Korea (3 pts, GD -1, GF 2). Key structural read: a DRAW sends both to 4 pts (Austria 2nd, Algeria 3rd on GD) -> a 4-pt third almost certainly qualifies, so a draw suits BOTH (biscotto incentive). A WIN takes the winner to 2nd (safe); the LOSER drops to 3rd on 3 pts and is then on the cut-line -- Austria losing by 1 keeps GD ~-1 (likely in on GF), Algeria losing falls to GD -2 or worse (likely out). So margins matter for the loser's third-place survival. Two evenly-matched sides (Austria 79, Algeria 76); Austria a slight edge by rating, GD, and a better win over Jordan. Home = Austria, away = Algeria. Cards not modelled (referee TBD).
_thirds_context: 8th/last qualifying third today = South Korea 3 pts, GD -1, GF 2 (Scotland 3/-3/1 is first out). Outcomes for the J third: (a) Austria win -> Algeria 3rd on 3 pts, GD <= -2 -> below the bar, likely OUT; (b) Algeria win -> Austria 3rd on 3 pts, GD -1 (lose by 1) with GF 3 -> edges South Korea on GF, likely IN, but GD -2+ (lose by 2+) -> OUT; (c) draw -> both 4 pts, Austria 2nd, Algeria 3rd on 4 pts -> almost certainly IN. K's third (still pending) competes for the same spots and can shift the cut-line by one.

meta.stakes: Winner takes 2nd and qualifies directly; the loser drops to 3rd and needs the best-thirds table (8th/last bar = South Korea 3 pts, GD -1, GF 2). A draw sends both to 4 pts (Austria 2nd, Algeria a near-safe 3rd) -- so a draw suits both. Evenly matched; Austria a slight edge.

home.name: Austria
notes: Rating 79; beat Jordan 3-1, competitive 0-2 loss to Argentina. attack 1.08, slight edge.
Must win for 2nd outright; a draw also leaves them 2nd on GD -> less desperate than Algeria.

away.name: Algeria
notes: Rating 76; beat Jordan 2-1, lost 0-3 to Argentina (GD -2). attack 1.0.
Most to play for: a win = 2nd; a draw = 3rd but a safe 4-pt third; a loss likely eliminates -> small structural lift, context 1.02.
